name: Gotcha Extreme Mini Mayhem
description: This gun/vest set is unique in that the vest can actually clip to the gun. This makes it similar to Tiger Toy's Lazer Tag gear. Mini Mayhem still maintains all the features that the Extreme gear has. I would probably buy one of these sets before a Gotcha set:)

specs.notes: This gun/vest set is unique in that the vest can actually clip to the gun. This makes it similar to Tiger Toy's Lazer Tag gear. Mini Mayhem still maintains all the features that the Extreme gear has. I would probably buy one of these sets before a Gotcha set:)

name: Gotcha Extreme Pistol
description: This gun is unique in that you can only reload when you are out of ammo. To reload you just tip the gun to the ground and pull the trigger. A very neat idea, if it was used in better equipment. The gun is also single fire only.

specs.notes: This gun is unique in that you can only reload when you are out of ammo. To reload you just tip the gun to the ground and pull the trigger. A very neat idea, if it was used in better equipment. The gun is also single fire only.

name: Gotcha Extreme Set
description: This set is really not as good as V2 or Radar gear. If possible buy either of those sets.

specs.contents: 2 (1 blue/1 red) Gotcha Extreme Vest s and 2 (1 blue/1 red) Gotcha Extreme Pistol s
specs.notes: This set is really not as good as V2 or Radar gear. If possible buy either of those sets.

name: Gotcha Extreme Vest
description: The vest will only work if it is plugged into the gun. There is no option for the back vest, and when you plug the gun in it enables Max Blasts for the gun. There is team A/B options for the vest and gun too.

specs.notes: The vest will only work if it is plugged into the gun. There is no option for the back vest, and when you plug the gun in it enables Max Blasts for the gun. There is team A/B options for the vest and gun too.

name: Radar Extreme Pistol
description: These guns are nice add-on's for V2 gear, but if you only play with Radar Extreme gear, you can use their team features. If you want to play on team A, you hold down the Arm button and turn it on, for team B, you hold down Reset button. If you play in team mode, your gun emits a constant IR that lets your opponent know if you have them targeted. It doesn't let you know you have a lock on them unless you can hear their vest. There is also no front light on this gun.

specs.notes: These guns are nice add-on's for V2 gear, but if you only play with Radar Extreme gear, you can use their team features. If you want to play on team A, you hold down the Arm button and turn it on, for team B, you hold down Reset button. If you play in team mode, your gun emits a constant IR that lets your opponent know if you have them targeted. It doesn't let you know you have a lock on them unless you can hear their vest. There is also no front light on this gun.

name: Radar Extreme Set
description: This set is good to add to your V2 arsenal. Has all the features V2 does with some extras you can use with only other Radar gear.

specs.contents: 2 (1 blue/1 red) Radar Extreme Vest s and 2 (1 blue/1 red) Radar Extreme Pistol s
specs.notes: This set is good to add to your V2 arsenal. Has all the features V2 does with some extras you can use with only other Radar gear.

name: Radar Extreme Vest
description: These sets are basically the same as the V2 vests, but they have the team ability like the Radar Pistols. The Blast Shield button is actually the blue/red circle right on the front, which is sometimes a problem if you lay down you activate your shield. The team A option is enabled by holding in the Blast Shield and turning it on, and the team B option is enabled by holding in the button on the back of the vest. The accessory port on this vest can use an Original Back Vest. This vest also takes hits slower then a V2 vest.

specs.notes: These sets are basically the same as the V2 vests, but they have the team ability like the Radar Pistols. The Blast Shield button is actually the blue/red circle right on the front, which is sometimes a problem if you lay down you activate your shield. The team A option is enabled by holding in the Blast Shield and turning it on, and the team B option is enabled by holding in the button on the back of the vest. The accessory port on this vest can use an Original Back Vest. This vest also takes hits slower then a V2 vest.

name: Team Force 250
description: This Team Force is very interesting. It uses Radar technology, but a V2 gun casing. It also has the Blast Shields and Max Blasts.

specs.notes: This Team Force is very interesting. It uses Radar technology, but a V2 gun casing. It also has the Blast Shields and Max Blasts.

name: Team Force 250 Set
description: This is a set similar to the Gotcha Extreme Mini Mayhem.

specs.notes: This is a set similar to the Gotcha Extreme Mini Mayhem.

name: Laser Attack Set
description: This was a re-release of the Laser Command set.

specs.contents: 1 Laser Command/Attack Pistol and 1 Laser Command/Attack Vest
specs.notes: This was a re-release of the Laser Command set.

name: Laser Command Hat
description: Neat idea, I want a Laser Challenge hat though:).

specs.notes: Neat idea, I want a Laser Challenge hat though:).

name: Laser Command Set
description: The basic one person set.

specs.contents: 1 Laser Command/Attack Pistol and 1 Laser Command/Attack Vest
specs.notes: The basic one person set.

name: Laser Command Stealth Atomizer
description: I personally have one of these and I love it. It has a very nice rifle feel, and has single/rapid fire options. I think this is a must get item if you use Laser Challenge gear.

specs.notes: I personally have one of these and I love it. It has a very nice rifle feel, and has single/rapid fire options. I think this is a must get item if you use Laser Challenge gear.

name: Laser Command Vest - Advanced
description: This vest comes with a back vest. A very wise idea.

specs.notes: This vest comes with a back vest. A very wise idea.

name: Laser Command/Attack Pistol
description: This is a nice short range gun.

specs.setNames: Laser Attack Set and Laser Command Set
specs.notes: This is a nice short range gun.

name: Laser Command/Attack Vest
description: This vest takes hits about the same speed as a V2 Vest, but doesn't allow a sensor to added. You can hardwire a Laser Challenge Back Vest to it if you have the knowledge.

specs.setNames: Laser Attack Set and Laser Command Set
specs.notes: This vest takes hits about the same speed as a V2 Vest, but doesn't allow a sensor to added. You can hardwire a Laser Challenge Back Vest to it if you have the knowledge.

name: Airmine & Explodroid
description: These expand the Laser Alley and make it a little more difficult. The reward for defeating one of these expansions is watching them blow off their bases and into the air.

specs.notes: These expand the Laser Alley and make it a little more difficult. The reward for defeating one of these expansions is watching them blow off their bases and into the air.

name: B.A.R.T. Droid
description: This little roaming target will help you improve your aim. It should be used on smooth floors.

specs.setNames: Dual Target Set and Tournament Set
specs.notes: This little roaming target will help you improve your aim. It should be used on smooth floors.

name: Back Vest
description: If you've got the money and the front vest, then get this! It really cuts down on cheating and makes the game harder, but more fun to play. Don't throw this away either! The Radar Extreme gear currently doesn't have a back vest, and this works with it.

specs.notes: If you've got the money and the front vest, then get this! It really cuts down on cheating and makes the game harder, but more fun to play. Don't throw this away either! The Radar Extreme gear currently doesn't have a back vest, and this works with it.

name: Capture The Flag
description: There are two timed modes, 30 seconds and 60 seconds. You can also not worry about the sounds and lights (indication of how long you have left to get the flag to the opposite base) and leave the batteries out.

specs.notes: There are two timed modes, 30 seconds and 60 seconds. You can also not worry about the sounds and lights (indication of how long you have left to get the flag to the opposite base) and leave the batteries out.

name: Cybersplash Pistol
description: Just a pistol that has a Cybersplash design.

specs.notes: Just a pistol that has a Cybersplash design.

name: Cybersplash Set
description: This vest is good for hot summer days. Each time you hit the opponents vest they are squirted with water. The sounds are low and hard to hear sometimes.

specs.notes: This vest is good for hot summer days. Each time you hit the opponents vest they are squirted with water. The sounds are low and hard to hear sometimes.

name: Cybersplash Vest
description: The Cybersplash vest has an attached back vest.

specs.notes: The Cybersplash vest has an attached back vest.

name: Dual Target Set
description: This set is similar to the Patrol Set, but offers a B.A.R.T. to entertain yourself if you have no one to play with.

specs.contents: 1 Front Vest , 1 Laser Pistol , and 1 B.A.R.T.
specs.notes: This set is similar to the Patrol Set, but offers a B.A.R.T. to entertain yourself if you have no one to play with.

name: EX-D Super Laser
description: The Extreme Distance (EX-D) Laser Blaster is a large improvement over the Laser Pistols, but it still has that annoying beep. The beep's on the EX-D's are longer and softer then the pistols, but are still there. The EX-D also has rapid fire, which the pistol does not have.

specs.setNames: Ex-treme Pak and Super Comp Set
specs.notes: The Extreme Distance (EX-D) Laser Blaster is a large improvement over the Laser Pistols, but it still has that annoying beep. The beep's on the EX-D's are longer and softer then the pistols, but are still there. The EX-D also has rapid fire, which the pistol does not have.

name: Ex-treme Pak
description: This is an interesting set. Good for adding more rifles to your Original gear arsenal.

specs.contents: 2 Front Vest s, 2 EX-D Rifles, and 1 Laser Trap
specs.notes: This is an interesting set. Good for adding more rifles to your Original gear arsenal.

name: Front Vest
description: The original Laser Challenge front vest is one of the most common pieces of Laser Challenge gear. A back vest can be plugged into it to add a new challenge to the game. The front vest receives hits slowly, and has a sensor adjuster on the front to increase/decrease sensitivity in bright light.

specs.setNames: Dual Target Set , Ex-treme Pak , Patrol Set , Super Comp Set , and Team Force Set
specs.notes: The original Laser Challenge front vest is one of the most common pieces of Laser Challenge gear. A back vest can be plugged into it to add a new challenge to the game. The front vest receives hits slowly, and has a sensor adjuster on the front to increase/decrease sensitivity in bright light.

name: Laser Alley
description: The Laser Alley allows you to play 5 different games with 20 different skill levels. You can also plug in Armine and Explodroid to increase the difficulty.

specs.notes: The Laser Alley allows you to play 5 different games with 20 different skill levels. You can also plug in Armine and Explodroid to increase the difficulty.

name: Laser Pistol
description: This is the original pistol, which doesn't fire very far and has a very annoying beep every 2 or 3 seconds. It has the standard reset button so you don't have to turn the vest on and off when you want to play again.

specs.setNames: Dual Target Set , Patrol Set , Team Force Set , and Tournament Set
specs.notes: This is the original pistol, which doesn't fire very far and has a very annoying beep every 2 or 3 seconds. It has the standard reset button so you don't have to turn the vest on and off when you want to play again.

name: Laser Trap
description: The Laser Trap has a 5 or 30 second delay mode. While it counts down it emits a buzzing sound. This is great for tossing at someone behind a structure or tree. This has a habit of resetting Laser Challenge gear that is more advanced then the original. This is best used with gear listed only on this page.

specs.notes: The Laser Trap has a 5 or 30 second delay mode. While it counts down it emits a buzzing sound. This is great for tossing at someone behind a structure or tree. This has a habit of resetting Laser Challenge gear that is more advanced then the original. This is best used with gear listed only on this page.

name: Light Warriors
description: An interesting Laser Challenge toy. You fire at each others robot trying to deactivate it. When you deactivate your opponents, it's head pops up and spins in circles.

specs.notes: An interesting Laser Challenge toy. You fire at each others robot trying to deactivate it. When you deactivate your opponents, it's head pops up and spins in circles.

name: Micromax Blaster
description: Small and compact, the vest and gun are small enough to fit in your pocket. The holster for the gun actually becomes a clip on vest. There is also a small extender that can boost the distance to about 150 ft. The nice thing about these guns also, they have a sound on/off just like the vest. So you can keep it quiet if you have to. To reload these guns all you have to do is hold the trigger in for about 2 seconds.

specs.batteryRequirements: 5 AAA's; 2 in the gun, 3 in the base.
specs.notes: Small and compact, the vest and gun are small enough to fit in your pocket. The holster for the gun actually becomes a clip on vest. There is also a small extender that can boost the distance to about 150 ft. The nice thing about these guns also, they have a sound on/off just like the vest. So you can keep it quiet if you have to. To reload these guns all you have to do is hold the trigger in for about 2 seconds.

name: Patrol Set
description: This was the first Laser Challenge product I bought. It was one of the first products released by Toymax along with the Team Force set.

specs.notes: This was the first Laser Challenge product I bought. It was one of the first products released by Toymax along with the Team Force set.

name: Shootback B.A.R.T.
description: He's back with a vengeance. BART has been upgraded to retaliate at all the people shooting at it's brother. This one also takes 10 hits to disable, but fires at you while you try to disable it.

specs.notes: He's back with a vengeance. BART has been upgraded to retaliate at all the people shooting at it's brother. This one also takes 10 hits to disable, but fires at you while you try to disable it.

name: Special Edition
description: This vest/gun combo seems to have a look similar to V2 gear, but it is just like the normal Team Set, just a different look. I'm not sure if it has any special features like Max Blast or Blast Shields.

specs.notes: This vest/gun combo seems to have a look similar to V2 gear, but it is just like the normal Team Set, just a different look. I'm not sure if it has any special features like Max Blast or Blast Shields.

name: Special Edition Set
description: Bascially another version of the original gear with a new look.

specs.notes: Bascially another version of the original gear with a new look.

name: Super Comp Set
description: This was a good starter set. If you had to use original gear find this style of gear.

specs.contents: 1 Front Vest , 1 Back Vest , and 1 EX-D
specs.notes: This was a good starter set. If you had to use original gear find this style of gear.

name: Team Force 2 Set
description: This set is more of a cross between Laser Challenge Pro and the Original gear. The guns fire 150 ft. which is longer then the original pistols. The color scheme is also different (yellow, blue, and grey).

specs.notes: This set is more of a cross between Laser Challenge Pro and the Original gear. The guns fire 150 ft. which is longer then the original pistols. The color scheme is also different (yellow, blue, and grey).

name: Team Force 2000 Set
description: Nearly the same as the Team Force 2 set.

specs.notes: Nearly the same as the Team Force 2 set.

name: Team Force Set
description: This set is good for two people just getting into Laser Challenge.

specs.contents: 2 Front Vest s and 2 Laser Pistol s
specs.notes: This set is good for two people just getting into Laser Challenge.

name: Tournament Set
description: I think the Dual Target set is better then this.

specs.notes: I think the Dual Target set is better then this.

name: Clash Pak
description: This Pak was cheaper then getting two of the single Pak's. This was not worth the investment due to Pro vests flawed designs.

specs.notes: This Pak was cheaper then getting two of the single Pak's. This was not worth the investment due to Pro vests flawed designs.

name: Competitor Pak
description: While really not a set, the Laser Challenge Pro had everything in one bundle, but still failed in the long run.

specs.notes: While really not a set, the Laser Challenge Pro had everything in one bundle, but still failed in the long run.

name: Laser Challenge Pro
description: This was a very good attempt at arena style gear, but alas it did not work out. The good things about it are that there are 3 sensors! One on the front, one on the back, and one on the gun. Also, there is a little red light on it, clearly visible to everyone else (except in extreme sunlight). It was an indicator to what team you were on (flashing or solid). It has 3 accessory ports which is awesome, but then only one accessory was made for it. The vest also vibrates when hit. Laser Challenge Pro is very faulty. Both of the ones I have purchased developed problems after about 2 months of use. The buttons started to fail or the digital display would stop working. I have done my best to repair them but without tearing the whole circuitry apart, there is no easy way to fix these problems. The Pro vest has a lot of features that lead to the Laser Challenge V2 gear, like Max Blasts, Shields, and playing with or without sound.

specs.notes: This was a very good attempt at arena style gear, but alas it did not work out. The good things about it are that there are 3 sensors! One on the front, one on the back, and one on the gun. Also, there is a little red light on it, clearly visible to everyone else (except in extreme sunlight). It was an indicator to what team you were on (flashing or solid). It has 3 accessory ports which is awesome, but then only one accessory was made for it. The vest also vibrates when hit. Laser Challenge Pro is very faulty. Both of the ones I have purchased developed problems after about 2 months of use. The buttons started to fail or the digital display would stop working. I have done my best to repair them but without tearing the whole circuitry apart, there is no easy way to fix these problems. The Pro vest has a lot of features that lead to the Laser Challenge V2 gear, like Max Blasts, Shields, and playing with or without sound.

name: Walkie Talkie
description: This is the only accessory that was released for the Laser Challenge Pro. You can hear the vest sound effects through them, and is a real nice way to talk to other people playing with Pro vests over great distances. They come in Red or Blue, another way to show what team you are on.

specs.notes: This is the only accessory that was released for the Laser Challenge Pro. You can hear the vest sound effects through them, and is a real nice way to talk to other people playing with Pro vests over great distances. They come in Red or Blue, another way to show what team you are on.

name: Lazer Pistol
description: The pistol comes with a scope and a shoulder rest. The sensor can also be attached to the pistol, probably adding limited hits to it. I was unable to test this with another due to having only one set at my disposal.

specs.notes: The pistol comes with a scope and a shoulder rest. The sensor can also be attached to the pistol, probably adding limited hits to it. I was unable to test this with another due to having only one set at my disposal.

name: Lock-On
description: This allows the sensor to be plugged into it and it can receive unlimited hits. When it is hit it has a falling bomb effect. It can be strapped to your body or cliped. Looks like you would mainly use this for target practice.

specs.notes: This allows the sensor to be plugged into it and it can receive unlimited hits. When it is hit it has a falling bomb effect. It can be strapped to your body or cliped. Looks like you would mainly use this for target practice.

name: Quick Shot Set
description: This set is the only set that I know for this gear. I wish I had access to two to test them out further.

specs.contents: 1 Lazer Pistol , 1 Electronic Target , and 1 Sensor
specs.notes: This set is the only set that I know for this gear. I wish I had access to two to test them out further.

name: Sensor
description: The sensor plugs into either the Electronic Target or Lazer Pistol, allowing either to be shot.

specs.notes: The sensor plugs into either the Electronic Target or Lazer Pistol, allowing either to be shot.

name: Lock-On
description: So far this gear is only available over seas. It has a lot of neat features. The headset has a visor that displays your hit points in a 7 segment LED. The visor can be rotated at will. The two triggers sound neat. The main one is in the usual place. If held, it produces a rapid fire effect (5 shots/sec). The second button is a 3 hit per one shot. It is called the High Power Shot (kind of like the Max Blast button on the Laser Challenge Gear, but you get 10 instead of 3 and you have 6 sec to shoot it before you loose it). The vest has 9 hits instead of 10 like LC. The team switches are neat. You decide between two teams. If you want to be on team A, you set the switch to it and turn on your gun. You do the same for team B.

specs.ammo: Single/Rapid fire(5 shots/sec), 10 high powered shots
specs.notes: So far this gear is only available over seas. It has a lot of neat features. The headset has a visor that displays your hit points in a 7 segment LED. The visor can be rotated at will. The two triggers sound neat. The main one is in the usual place. If held, it produces a rapid fire effect (5 shots/sec). The second button is a 3 hit per one shot. It is called the High Power Shot (kind of like the Max Blast button on the Laser Challenge Gear, but you get 10 instead of 3 and you have 6 sec to shoot it before you loose it). The vest has 9 hits instead of 10 like LC. The team switches are neat. You decide between two teams. If you want to be on team A, you set the switch to it and turn on your gun. You do the same for team B.

name: Lock-On 2
description: This unit is slightly shorter and a little thicker than the original. The two are fully compatible. The Hi Power Shot is a little bit harder to press. The headset is more compact which makes it sit to one side of your head. A bit ackward because it is thicker and has a more definate square shape to it.

specs.ammo: Single/Rapid fire(5 shots/sec), 10 high powered shots
specs.notes: This unit is slightly shorter and a little thicker than the original. The two are fully compatible. The Hi Power Shot is a little bit harder to press. The headset is more compact which makes it sit to one side of your head. A bit ackward because it is thicker and has a more definate square shape to it.

name: Adapter Pak
description: Adapters were sold in packs, not single.

specs.notes: Adapters were sold in packs, not single.

name: ELS Adapter
description: The ELS adapter links the gun to the vest, and enables a new style of playing.

specs.setNames: Adapter Pak and ELS Game System
specs.notes: The ELS adapter links the gun to the vest, and enables a new style of playing.

name: ELS Computer
description: The ELS computer adds Laser Challenge Pro like features to the V2 line. When you first turn it on you can program the time limit (up to 10 hours), number of hits (up to 30), and how many are on each team (red and blue). Each person plugs in when it asks and everyone gets a unique ID. When the game is over, whether time ran out or one whole team is dead, everyone plugs back in and it pulls up various statistics like hit % for each person, who killed who, the best shooter, and the worst shooter. You cannot shoot your teammates or yourself and you have a limited amount of reloads. You just plug back into the computer to ge t more reloads. The only down side is there can only be a maximum of 8 people. There is also ELS software to connect with a computer and print out the results.

specs.notes: The ELS computer adds Laser Challenge Pro like features to the V2 line. When you first turn it on you can program the time limit (up to 10 hours), number of hits (up to 30), and how many are on each team (red and blue). Each person plugs in when it asks and everyone gets a unique ID. When the game is over, whether time ran out or one whole team is dead, everyone plugs back in and it pulls up various statistics like hit % for each person, who killed who, the best shooter, and the worst shooter. You cannot shoot your teammates or yourself and you have a limited amount of reloads. You just plug back into the computer to ge t more reloads. The only down side is there can only be a maximum of 8 people. There is also ELS software to connect with a computer and print out the results.

name: ELS Game System
description: This set is the basic begining to the V2 ELS System.

specs.contents: 1 ELS Computer and 2 ELS Adapter s(1 Blue and 1 Red)
specs.notes: This set is the basic begining to the V2 ELS System.

name: Firestorm
description: This is one of the best Laser Challenge guns out there. With an insane range on firing, it takes a while to learn how to aim this beast correctly. It contains a scope that assists you in aiming. Omega was constructed out of a Firestorm.

specs.batteryRequirements: 6 AA's (take out front 3 to disable recoil)
specs.notes: This is one of the best Laser Challenge guns out there. With an insane range on firing, it takes a while to learn how to aim this beast correctly. It contains a scope that assists you in aiming. Omega was constructed out of a Firestorm.

name: Solo Gear
description: The set for one person.

specs.notes: The set for one person.

name: Twin Gear
description: I think this set was more common then the Solo Gear.

specs.contents: 2 V2 Vest s and 2 V2 Pistol s
specs.notes: I think this set was more common then the Solo Gear.

name: Ultrawide
description: This is a nice rifle style gun. It has a nice aiming scope and is very easy to aim. You can also activate Ultrawide mode, which spreads the barrels and gives you a small area that you can hit people. This mode is hard to use and normal mode is preferred by most.

specs.ammo: 25 Normal/1 shot every 5 seconds on Ultrawide
specs.notes: This is a nice rifle style gun. It has a nice aiming scope and is very easy to aim. You can also activate Ultrawide mode, which spreads the barrels and gives you a small area that you can hit people. This mode is hard to use and normal mode is preferred by most.

name: Ultrawide Blaster Set
description: This is a good set if you want to use a rifle instead of a pistol.

specs.notes: This is a good set if you want to use a rifle instead of a pistol.

name: V2 Pistol
description: This is a very nice pistol. Easy to hold and aim, usually you can fire two of these at once and get some nice hits in.

specs.notes: This is a very nice pistol. Easy to hold and aim, usually you can fire two of these at once and get some nice hits in.

name: V2 Vest
description: The V2 Vests are nice in that they take hits quickly (10 hits in about 6 seconds) and they have elastic bands to keep them positioned properly. The back vest is hard wired to the front vest, and the port on the front vest is for the ELS system. There is a 9th hit sound which gives you about 3 seconds, and a 10th hit sound which makes it known you are dead.

specs.setNames: Solo Gear , Twin Gear , and Ultrawide Blaster Set
specs.notes: The V2 Vests are nice in that they take hits quickly (10 hits in about 6 seconds) and they have elastic bands to keep them positioned properly. The back vest is hard wired to the front vest, and the port on the front vest is for the ELS system. There is a 9th hit sound which gives you about 3 seconds, and a 10th hit sound which makes it known you are dead.

name: Voice Command Lock-On
description: Not much is known about this Lock-On. It seems to have been released mainly in Japan. I found a small section on a message board posted by Peter: It has 3 modes Voice command Without Voice commands Original The voice command instructions are complicated and it does not work well in reality you can still use the gear without the annoying temperamental voice commands To turn on the gun hold down the display button (square) and the mode button (round) at the same time while turning the gun to either Team A or B The gun will say Full Power (equals 100 hit points) You get 50 shots in blast mode before a 5 second automatic reload cycle starts. During the reload you may not shoot, but you may be tagged. You get 25 shots in hyper mode before reloading Change modes by pressing the mode button In Blast mode you do 6 points each hit In hyper mode you do 12 points each hit to a target in blast mode, but 20 points to a target who is also in hyper mode In other words if all players are in blast mode it takes 17 hits to take out an opponent but with everyone in Hyper mode it only take 5 hits to eliminate a target There is also a destroy mode that becomes available after you get shot down to 40 points or less. It allows you to do 40 points of damage to your target in one shot, but then your gun goes through the 5 second reload feature. Basically it is a desperate feature and not very useful To active the original game features hold down the display button while turning the on switch to team A or B The gun will count up to 9 hit points and count down to 0 with each tag unlimited ammo, no reload cycle the Mode button becomes a "triple shot" button. you get a maximum of 10 triple shots. once you press the button the gun beep quickly for 6 seconds. If the gun is not fired before the 6 seconds is up the grenade is "lost" Oh yeah, the guns beep constantly and can not be stopped. If you hold the trigger down it will be quieted to a "click" sound. You are limited to team A vs. team B games and can not play everyman for himself type games you can not shot your self or team mates the Lock on feature lets you scan the woods and the target gun will beep loudly when you are aiming at it (this would be a good time to pull the trigger, or if you are the target that is beeping, DUCK!

specs.notes: Not much is known about this Lock-On. It seems to have been released mainly in Japan. I found a small section on a message board posted by Peter: It has 3 modes Voice command Without Voice commands Original The voice command instructions are complicated and it does not work well in reality you can still use the gear without the annoying temperamental voice commands To turn on the gun hold down the display button (square) and the mode button (round) at the same time while turning the gun to either Team A or B The gun will say Full Power (equals 100 hit points) You get 50 shots in blast mode before a 5 second automatic reload cycle starts. During the reload you may not shoot, but you may be tagged. You get 25 shots in hyper mode before reloading Change modes by pressing the mode button In Blast mode you do 6 points each hit In hyper mode you do 12 points each hit to a target in blast mode, but 20 points to a target who is also in hyper mode In other words if all players are in blast mode it takes 17 hits to take out an opponent but with everyone in Hyper mode it only take 5 hits to eliminate a target There is also a destroy mode that becomes available after you get shot down to 40 points or less. It allows you to do 40 points of damage to your target in one shot, but then your gun goes through the 5 second reload feature. Basically it is a desperate feature and not very useful To active the original game features hold down the display button while turning the on switch to team A or B The gun will count up to 9 hit points and count down to 0 with each tag unlimited ammo, no reload cycle the Mode button becomes a "triple shot" button. you get a maximum of 10 triple shots. once you press the button the gun beep quickly for 6 seconds. If the gun is not fired before the 6 seconds is up the grenade is "lost" Oh yeah, the guns beep constantly and can not be stopped. If you hold the trigger down it will be quieted to a "click" sound. You are limited to team A vs. team B games and can not play everyman for himself type games you can not shot your self or team mates the Lock on feature lets you scan the woods and the target gun will beep loudly when you are aiming at it (this would be a good time to pull the trigger, or if you are the target that is beeping, DUCK!
